format.unitName: Thematic Unit Name or chapters group
chapters: Specific chapters/topics allocated to this unit in this term

messages.content: Return only valid JSON that matches the requested schema. Do not include markdown or explanations.

Given the following curriculum structure, divide the units and topics into exactly 3 distinct academic terms (e.g., Term 1, Term 2, etc. depending on termCount 3).
    Allocate marks weightage realistically (e.g. totaling out of 100 across terms, with approximate weights: e.g. 20, 25, 30 marks per unit depends on size).
    Make sure EVERY unit and sub-topic from the original curriculum is covered across the terms.
 
    Curriculum:
    {
  "subject": "Mathematics",
  "gradeLevel": "9 / IX",
  "overallDescription": "6 units extracted from the curriculum source using staged structural extraction.",
  "coreObjectives": [
    "Understand the concept of a rational number.",
    "Represent rational numbers on the number line.",
    "Understand the properties of rational numbers.",
    "Explain the concept of density of rational numbers.",
    "Compute decimal representation of rational numbers.",
    "Generate decimal expansions systematically.",
    "Understand the concept of irrational numbers.",
    "Prove the irrationality.",
    "Construct the square root spiral.",
    "Apply computational thinking to represent rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models.",
    "Understand the meaning of an algebraic expression.",
    "Define a polynomial."
  ],
  "units": [
    {
      "unitId": "U1",
      "unitName": "Number System",
      "description": "Number System",
      "topics": [
        "Introduction to rational numbers",
        "Representation of rational numbers on the number line",
        "Density of rational numbers and its proof",
        "Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers",
        "Decimal representation of rational numbers",
        "Introduction to irrational numbers",
        "Proof of irrationality of √2 and √3",
        "The square root spiral"
      ]
    },
    {
      "unitId": "U2",
      "unitName": "Algebra",
      "description": "Introduction to Polynomials, Sequences and Progressions, Exploring Algebraic Identities, Linear Equations in Two Variables",
      "topics": [
        "Algebraic expressions",
        "Definition of a polynomial.",
        "Degree of a polynomial",
        "Introduction to linear polynomials and applications",
        "Exploring linear patterns",
        "Modelling linear growth and linear decay",
        "Linear relationships",
        "Visualising linear relationships",
        "Slope and y-intercept of a line y = ax + b",
        "Introduction to sequences",
        "Explicit or general rule of a sequence",
        "Recursive rule of a sequence",
        "Arithmetic Progressions (AP): nth term, visualising an AP, and practical contexts leading to Aps",
        "Sum of the first n natural numbers",
        "Geometric Progressions (GP): nth term, visualising a GP, and practical contexts leading to GPs",
        "Applications of GP in fractals",
        "Tower of Hanoi puzzle",
        "Revisiting algebraic identities",
        "Visualising identities using geometrical models",
        "Factorisation of algebraic expressions using identities",
        "More identities and their applications",
        "Visualising factorisation of quadratic expressions through algebra tiles and without using algebra tiles",
        "Finding new identities",
        "Simplifying rational expressions",
        "Introduction to linear equations in two variables through practical examples",
        "Solution of linear equation in two variables: graphical representation",
        "Slope-intercept form of linear equation in two variables",
        "Drawing graphs of linear equations when x and y assume only certain values",
        "Pair of linear equations in two variables",
        "Graphical method for solving a pair of linear equations in two variables",
        "Nature of solutions: consistency and inconsistency",
        "Algebraic methods of solving a pair of linear equations: substitution and elimination method"
      ]
    },
    {
      "unitId": "U3",
      "unitName": "Coordinate Geometry",
      "description": "Coordinate Geometry",
      "topics": [
        "Brief history of coordinate geometry",
        "The 2-D Cartesian coordinate system",
        "Distance between two points in the 2-D plane",
        "Midpoint of the line- segment between two points in the 2-D plane"
      ]
    },
    {
      "unitId": "U4",
      "unitName": "Geometry",
      "description": "Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates, Lines and Angles, Triangles – Congruence Theorems, 4-gons (Quadrilaterals), Circles",
      "topics": [
        "History of geometry",
        "Constructing a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras",
        "Discovering Euclid's definitions",
        "Axioms: Axioms of measurement and rules for geometric objects",
        "Rays and angles",
        "Measures of angles",
        "Intersecting lines and angles",
        "Pairs of angles",
        "Theorems and examples on intersecting lines",
        "Theorems and examples on parallel lines",
        "Practical applications of triangles",
        "Proofs of conditions of congruence of triangles",
        "Theorems on triangles",
        "Propositions and their converse",
        "Problems based on applications of theorems on triangles",
        "Properties of parallelograms",
        "Important theorems related to parallelograms and their proof",
        "Midpoint theorem and its applications",
        "Understanding the notion of central symmetry in the context of parallelograms",
        "Practical applications and uses of circles",
        "Definitions related to a circle — centre, diameter, and radius",
        "Chords and the angles they subtend",
        "Midpoints and perpendicular bisectors of chords",
        "Distance of chords from the centre",
        "Subtended angles by an arc",
        "Cyclicity of points"
      ]
    },
    {
      "unitId": "U5",
      "unitName": "Mensuration",
      "description": "Area and Perimeter, Surface Area and Volume",
      "topics": [
        "Perimeter of shapes",
        "Perimeter of a circle: Introduction to Pi and its irrationality",
        "Length of an arc",
        "Area of shapes: rectangles, parallelograms, and triangles",
        "Heron's formula",
        "Squaring a rectangle: Proof from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras",
        "Area of a circle: derivation",
        "Area of the sector of a circle",
        "Brahmagupta's formula for area of a cyclic 4-gon",
        "Heron's formula as a special case of Brahmagupta's formula",
        "Surface areas and volumes of spheres (including hemispheres) and right circular cones",
        "Compute the surface area and volume of a cuboid.",
        "Describe a right circular cylinder using its radius and height.",
        "Recognise cones in daily life, and describe them using radius and height.",
        "Recognise a pyramid, and identify its base and apex."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unitId": "U6",
      "unitName": "Statistics and Probability",
      "description": "Statistics, Introduction to Probability",
      "topics": [
        "Graphical representation of data",
        "Measures of central tendency",
        "Concept of probability and randomness",
        "The probability scale",
        "Empirical probability: analysing statistical data and performing experiments",
        "Theoretical probability: sample space and events",
        "Representing probability through tree diagrams and tables"
      ]
    }
  ],

    "competencies": {
      "competency_groups": [
        {
          "code": "C-1.1",
          "title": "Understanding of Numbers and Real Number Set Properties",
          "description": "Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.",
          "competencies": [
            "Understand the concept of a rational number.",
            "Represent rational numbers on the number line.",
            "Understand the properties of rational numbers.",
            "Explain the concept of density of rational numbers.",
            "Compute decimal representation of rational numbers.",
            "Understand the concept of irrational numbers.",
            "Prove the irrationality.",
            "Construct the square root spiral."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Number System",
          "chapter_name": "Number System"
        },
        {
          "code": "C-3.2, C-8.1",
          "title": "Modelling Linear Growth/Decay and Identifying Patterns",
          "description": "Models linear growth and decay using linear polynomials; identifies patterns in linear relationships.",
          "competencies": [
            "Understand the meaning of an algebraic expression.",
            "Define a polynomial.",
            "Identify the degree, terms and coefficients of terms in a polynomial.",
            "Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials.",
            "Explain and identify patterns in linear relationships.",
            "Identify the slope and y-intercept of a linear equation in two variables."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Algebra",
          "chapter_name": "Introduction to Polynomials"
        },
        {
          "code": "C-8.1, C-9.2",
          "title": "Sequences and Progressions: Rules and Applications",
          "description": "Identifies patterns in sequences; determines recursive and explicit rules for APs and GPs.",
          "competencies": [
            "Understand the concept of a sequence of numbers.",
            "Identify the pattern in a sequence and predict the next few terms.",
            "Determine the recursive and explicit rules for different sequences.",
            "Obtain the terms of sequence given its recursive and explicit rule.",
            "Identify Arithmetic Progressions (AP).",
            "Determine the nth term of an AP.",
            "Visualise an AP graphically.",
            "Identify Geometric Progressions (GP).",
            "Determine the nth term of a GP.",
            "Visualise a GP graphically."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Algebra",
          "chapter_name": "Sequences and Progressions"
        },
        {
          "code": "C-7.2, C-9.1",
          "title": "Visualising Algebraic Identities and Factorisation",
          "description": "Uses computational thinking to visualise algebraic identities using geometric models.",
          "competencies": [
            "Visualise algebraic identities using geometric models.",
            "Determine the factors of algebraic expressions using identities.",
            "Interpret factors of quadratic expressions through geometric models.",
            "Find simplified versions of rational expressions."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Algebra",
          "chapter_name": "Exploring Algebraic Identities"
        },
        {
          "code": "C-3.2, C-8.1, C-9.4",
          "title": "Solving Linear Equations and Modelling Contexts",
          "description": "Models daily-life phenomena using representations; solves pairs of linear equations graphically and algebraically.",
          "competencies": [
            "Understand the concept of a linear equation in two variables.",
            "Graph a pair of linear equations.",
            "Solve a pair of linear equations graphically.",
            "Solve a pair of linear equations through the methods of substitution and elimination.",
            "Determine the nature of solutions of a pair of linear equations."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Algebra",
          "chapter_name": "Linear Equations in Two Variables"
        },
        {
          "code": "C-4.5, C-9.1",
          "title": "Coordinate Geometry: Location and Distance",
          "description": "Specifies locations using coordinates; computes distance between points.",
          "competencies": [
            "Specify locations and the position of one point relative to another point using coordinates.",
            "Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates.",
            "Compute the distance between two points using coordinates.",
            "Determine whether three points lie in a straight line using coordinates."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Coordinate Geometry",
          "chapter_name": "Coordinate Geometry"
        },
        {
          "code": "C-7.1, C-7.3",
          "title": "Euclid's Axioms and Geometric Constructions",
          "description": "Proves mathematical statements using axioms; constructs geometric shapes.",
          "competencies": [
            "Describe how geometry grew from the practical needs ancient civilisations.",
            "Understand the role of definitions, axioms, and postulates.",
            "State the 5 postulates of Euclidean geometry.",
            "Define parallelism of straight lines."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Geometry",
          "chapter_name": "Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates"
        },
        {
          "code": "C-7.1, C-9.2",
          "title": "Lines and Angles: Theorems and Reasoning",
          "description": "Follows proof by contradiction; applies computational thinking to analyse geometric ideas.",
          "competencies": [
            "Explain the notion of an angle and a ray.",
            "Classify angles as acute, right, obtuse, or reflex.",
            "State and apply the linear pair theorem and its converse.",
            "Prove that vertically opposite angles are equal."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Geometry",
          "chapter_name": "Lines and Angles"
        },
        {
          "code": "C-4.1, C-7.3",
          "title": "Triangle Congruence Theorems",
          "description": "Identifies uses of triangle rigidity; proves conditions for congruence.",
          "competencies": [
            "Explain that a triangle is rigid.",
            "Describe what it means for two triangles to be congruent.",
            "Use the SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, and AAS congruence conditions."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Geometry",
          "chapter_name": "Triangles – Congruence Theorems"
        },
        {
          "code": "C-4.2, C-7.3",
          "title": "Quadrilaterals: Properties and Symmetry",
          "description": "Proves characterisations of parallelograms; understands central symmetry.",
          "competencies": [
            "Frame a precise definition of a 4-gon.",
            "Prove various characterisations of a parallelogram.",
            "Prove the midpoint theorem."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Geometry",
          "chapter_name": "4-gons (Quadrilaterals)"
        },
        {
          "code": "C-7.3, C-9.1",
          "title": "Circle Geometry and Properties",
          "description": "Explains relationships between chords, arcs, and angles; constructs circumcircles.",
          "competencies": [
            "State the definition of a circle.",
            "Explain why there exists a unique circle through three non-collinear points.",
            "Construct the circumcircle and circumcentre of a triangle."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Geometry",
          "chapter_name": "Circles"
        },
        {
          "code": "C-5.1, C-9.3",
          "title": "Area Calculations: Heron's and Brahmagupta's Formulas",
          "description": "Visualises area of triangles using Heron's formula; understands generalisation.",
          "competencies": [
            "Define perimeter as the length around the boundary of any shape.",
            "Compute the circumference of a circle and the length of an arc."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Mensuration",
          "chapter_name": "Area and Perimeter"
        },
        {
          "code": "C-5.1, C-9.4",
          "title": "Surface Area and Volume Calculations",
          "description": "Computes surface area and volume of solid objects; uses computational thinking to compare shapes.",
          "competencies": [
            "Compute the surface area and volume of a cuboid.",
            "Describe a right circular cylinder using its radius and height.",
            "Recognise cones in daily life, and describe them using radius and height."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Mensuration",
          "chapter_name": "Surface Area and Volume"
        },
        {
          "code": "C-6.1, C-9.3",
          "title": "Statistical Data Analysis",
          "description": "Collects, organises, visualise and interpret data; computes weighted average.",
          "competencies": [
            "Compute and apply weighted average in different settings.",
            "Read and interpret stacked bar graphs and 100% stacked bar graphs."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Statistics and Probability",
          "chapter_name": "Statistics"
        },
        {
          "code": "C-6.2, C-9.3",
          "title": "Probability: Empirical and Theoretical Concepts",
          "description": "Estimates empirical probability; applies theoretical probability definitions.",
          "competencies": [
            "Understand the concept of randomness.",
            "Describe the likelihood of an event using the probability scale.",
            "Define theoretical probability of an event."
          ],
          "unit_name": "Statistics and Probability",
          "chapter_name": "Introduction to Probability"
        }
      ]
    },
